headlines.title: Putin visits military installation, vowing to take more of Ukraine
headlines.summary: The Russian president donned military fatigues as he tried to counter a narrative that Moscow is stumbling in its war after Ukrainian drone strikes set off an acute fuel shortage.

headlines.title: In Tehran, huge throngs mourn assassinated supreme leader Ali Khamenei
headlines.summary: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who ruled over the Islamic Republic of Iran for 37 years, was killed along with several family members in an airstrike in February.

headlines.title: On July Fourth, Pope Leo urges U.S. to welcome, protect and assist immigrants
headlines.summary: In Lampedusa, island gateway to Europe, the U.S.-born pope stressed human dignity and told America: "in every generation" immigrants "helped to shape the nation’s character.”

headlines.title: Iran’s leader was killed in February. The country will finally bury him.
headlines.summary: For the surviving Iranian regime, the funeral offers an opportunity to project power after withstanding months of war with Israel and the United States.

headlines.title: In a hit low-budget film, Beijing sees a way to court Chinese communities abroad
headlines.summary: “Dear You,” a Teochew-language family drama and migration story, has raised fraught questions of identity among some Chinese communities abroad.

headlines.title: A name, a document, a future: Cameroon’s fight to register every child
headlines.summary: Efforts to expand birth registration in Cameroon are gaining ground, but millions of children remain undocumented.

headlines.title: Severe weather disrupts US’s 250th celebrations
headlines.summary: President Trump describes milestone as 'one of the most joyous and glorious' in US's history in campaign style speech.

headlines.title: This immigrant served in the US military. Now he faces deportation
headlines.summary: Benito Miranda Hernandez completed three tours during the Iraq war but now faces removal from the US to Mexico.

headlines.title: Huge crowds mourn Iran’s Khamenei; Trump hints at Netanyahu meeting
headlines.summary: Iran's late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and members of his family were killed in a US-Israeli air strike in February.

headlines.title: White nationalists march in Washington, DC, area during July 4 festivities
headlines.summary: Donald Trump has faced condemnation for failing to forcefully reject white nationalists during his presidency.

headlines.title: In Mount Rushmore speech, Trump veers from U.S. exceptionalism to warnings about communism
headlines.summary: President Trump ushered in America's 250th anniversary with a darkly political speech that swerved from the typically apolitical, unifying speeches past presidents have given to mark Independence Day.

headlines.title: Trump addresses nation and fireworks light up National Mall after storm delay
headlines.summary: President Trump lauded America's storied history on Saturday night in a speech marking the 250th anniversary of its independence, while also praising his war in Iran, railing against "communists," and vowing to restrict mail-in ballots.

headlines.title: Move over Chutes & Ladders: Schisto & Ladders has educational value plus worms
headlines.summary: Spread by parasitic worms that can infect via a splash of water, schistosomiasis causes debilitating symptoms. It hits kids hard. A board game teaches how to prevent it.

headlines.title: China's military promotes 2 new generals after anti-corruption purge thins ranks
headlines.summary: The shake-up is believed to be an effort to ensure the military's loyalty to the Communist Party and its leader, Xi Jinping.

headlines.title: Opinion: America is still a work in progress
headlines.summary: Poets like Walt Whitman and Langston Hughes have written movingly about America.

headlines.title: Heat dome brings extreme weather to North America
headlines.summary: OTTAWA/NEW YORK — A destructive "heat dome" is bringing extreme heat to parts of North America, including large areas of Canada and the northeastern United States, while severe storms and torrential rain have caused flooding in Canada ahead of Canada Day and US Independence Day celebrations.

headlines.title: Russia, Ukraine trade strikes as peace efforts stall
headlines.summary: MOSCOW/KYIV — Russia and Ukraine continued aerial attacks on each other, officials from both sides said, as the fate of ending the conflict remained uncertain.

headlines.title: Japan-Philippines sea talks plan slammed
headlines.summary: A Chinese think tank has released a legal opinion condemning the decision of Japan and the Philippines to initiate maritime delimitation talks related to the waters east of China's Taiwan Island, saying that the move "constitutes an internationally wrongful act" because it bypasses China, which is a direct stakeholder.

headlines.title: Google loses appeal over record fine
headlines.summary: The European Union's top court dismissed an appeal on Thursday from the United States technology company Google against a 4.34-billion-euro ($4.95 billion) antitrust fine, setting up a likely protracted dispute between Brussels and Washington.

headlines.title: Legacy of Silk Road shapes cultural ties
headlines.summary: China and Turkiye have mutually influenced each other's cultures over time, building a substantial heritage legacy and great potential for future cultural cooperation, a forum has heard.

headlines.title: Eknath Shinde admitted to hospital, condition stable
headlines.summary: Maharashtra Deputy Chief Minister Eknath Shinde is hospitalized due to viral fever and exhaustion, but his condition is stable.

headlines.title: Search for two LeT terrorists enters second day in J&K's Shopian
headlines.summary: The anti-terror operation in J&K's Shopian enters day two. Security forces hunt for cornered Lashkar-e-Taiba terrorists Zakir Ganie and Latif Bhat.

headlines.title: Monsoon tracker July 5 LIVE: Mumbai wakes up to brief respite after heavy rain; IMD issues red alert for more showers
headlines.summary: Weather today LIVE: Get updates on Mumbai rain, traffic, train services, and IMD weather forecasts as heavy showers continue unabated across the city.

headlines.title: Two killed as two-storey building collapses in Chandigarh industrial area
headlines.summary: Two people died in a building collapse in Chandigarh's industrial area, prompting a major rescue operation by emergency services.

headlines.title: Another batch of pilgrims on way to Amarnath shrine
headlines.summary: Over 6,700 pilgrims, including women and children, embark on the Amarnath Yatra from Jammu under tight security.

headlines.title: Trump offers to help Putin find deal with Ukraine, also speaks with Zelenskiy
headlines.summary: Reuters: Trump offers to help Putin find deal with Ukraine, also speaks with Zelenskiy.

headlines.title: Masked Patriot Front white nationalists stage July 4 march through DC
headlines.summary: Reuters: Masked Patriot Front white nationalists stage July 4 march through DC.

headlines.title: As Venezuela death toll rises, survivor recounts days beneath the rubble
headlines.summary: Reuters: As Venezuela death toll rises, survivor recounts days beneath the rubble.

headlines.title: Israel hasn't said who its soldiers abused. Two Gaza mothers think it's their son
headlines.summary: Reuters: Israel hasn't said who its soldiers abused. Two Gaza mothers think it's their son.

headlines.title: Prince Harry's wife Meghan and their children will not join him in London next week
headlines.summary: Reuters: Prince Harry's wife Meghan and their children will not join him in London next week.

hackerNews.title: Jellyfish can heal wounds in minutes. Scientists want their secrets
hackerNews.summary: A decade ago this summer, at the Marine Biological Laboratory, Jocelyn Malamy watched jellyfish cells “walk” toward each other to close a wound for the first time. An associate professor in Molecular Genetics and Cell Biology at the University of Chicago, Malamy had received transparent, dime-sized medusae of the species Clytia hemisphaerica from Evelyn Houliston’s lab at the Marine Observatoire in Villefranche.

summarySections.title: News and markets
sentences: Global headlines: Putin visits military installation, vowing to take more of Ukraine (The Russian president donned military fatigues as he tried to counter a narrative that Moscow is stumbling in its war after Ukrainian drone strikes set off an acute fuel shortage); In Tehran, huge throngs mourn assassinated supreme leader Ali Khamenei (Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who ruled over the Islamic Republic of Iran for 37 years, was killed along with several family members in an airstrike in February); and On July Fourth, Pope Leo urges U.S. to welcome, protect and assist immigrants (In Lampedusa, island gateway to Europe, the U.S.-born pope stressed human dignity and told America: "in every generation" immigrants "helped to shape the nation’s character.”).
Markets leaned higher, with 5 of 7 tracked indexes up; Nikkei 225 up 1.47% was the largest move.
Benchmarks tracked: S&P 500, NASDAQ Composite, Euronext 100 Index, and Public Joint-Stock Company Moscow Exchange MICEX-RTS plus 3 more indexes.

summarySections.title: Hacker News and Product Hunt
sentences: Hacker News: Shadcn/UI now defaults to Base UI instead of Radix (Latest updates and announcements); Command and Conquer Generals natively ported to macOS, iPhone, iPad using Fable (Command & Conquer Generals: Zero Hour running natively on macOS, iPhone & iPad — real engine (EA GPL v3 source, via GeneralsX),...); and If you're a button, you have one job (A blog about software craft and quality).

Product Hunt features CentryAI (Subscription tracker built by someone who forgot 11 of them), ChecklistFox (AI checklist maker for beautiful pdfs, free & instant), and Termi Protocol (Watch your AI coding agents build, live in 3D).
